channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Efforts by Georgia lawmakers to legalize sports betting fizzle again
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — It turns out 2025 isn't the year for legalized sports betting in Georgia. Efforts to send a state constitutional amendment to voters failed Thursday in the state legislature, as neither the amendment nor a bill laying out details ever came to a vote in the House.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: A San Carlos Apache teen who was reported missing and then found dead marks one of the latest tragedies as Native American communities continue to grapple with the epidemic of people being killed or going missing at high rates.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Black box of ambulance jet that crashed in Philly wasn't recording audio, likely hadn't worked for years: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder on a plane that crash-landed in Philadelphia was not working and apparently did not work for several years, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico adult dies with measles, though cause of death isn't yet confirmed
item.description: An adult who was infected with measles has died in New Mexico, though the virus has not been confirmed as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Devi Shastri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's move to restore old Army base names highlights Nebraska man's heroics at 18 in World War I
item.description: For more than a century, Fort Benning’s name honored a Confederate general who supported slavery. The military changed the name of the Army base in Georgia two years ago, but now the Trump administration is set on restoring the familiar one — this time for a different Benning.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Why Missouri, Kansas pet owners should be on the watch for coyote encounters
item.description: February is the peak of breeding season for coyotes and continues into March, according to the Missouri Department of Conservation.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio State Highway Patrol supervisors punished after reported abuse
item.description: What began as an investigation into one man resulted in repercussions for two additional men in positions of power as the investigative process progressed.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, NewsNation

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board says a cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that crashed in Philadelphia in January and likely had not been working for several years.
item.dc:creator: Maryclaire Dale, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Dog beaten, dragged, thrown off bridge to its death
item.description: A nearby business owner, who did not want to be identified, said he watched the security video after he saw the dead dog in the parking lot.
item.dc:creator: Raven Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Bill to pay troops during shutdown filed as funding deadline looms
item.description: Rep. Jen Kiggans (R-Va.) reintroduced a bill Thursday to ensure that members of the military get paid during a government shutdown, a move that comes as a March 14 deadline ticks closer without a deal to extend funding.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: 3 skiers missing after avalanche hits remote mountain range in Alaska
item.description: Alaska State Troopers said three skiers are likely dead after an avalanche buried them in the Chugach Mountains near Girdwood, Alaska, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers and government officials in a broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Twelve Chinese nationals — including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company — have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and DAKE KANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 3 skiers buried in Alaska avalanche, officials say
item.description: An avalanche in the Alaska backcountry swept away and buried three skiers who were believed to be dead under a pile of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: By MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two charged in Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths 14 months after bodies found frozen in backyard
item.description: Two men have been arrested in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found frozen in the backyard of a Kansas City, Missouri, area home back in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tom Llamas to succeed Lester Holt as NBC "Nightly News" anchor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NBC News appointed Tom Llamas on Wednesday to replace Lester Holt as anchor of “Nightly News” starting this summer, but he's not abandoning his streaming newscast. The elevation of Llamas, 45, was widely anticipated.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Marines and soldiers discharged for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine show limited interest in rejoining
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Roughly 650 Marines and Army soldiers who were forced out of the military for refusing to get the COVID-19 vaccine have so far expressed interest in returning to the force under an executive order from President Donald Trump, officials said this week.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: VIDEO: "This one decided to jump on board": 900-pound dolphin lands on boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
